tldr: Transformer removes recurrence and convolution, using attention alone to improve parallelism while reaching strong translation quality.
research_question: Can sequence transduction be modeled using attention alone, without recurrent or convolutional layers?
contributions: Defines scaled dot-product and multi-head attention.
Demonstrates faster training and strong WMT 2014 translation results.
method: The encoder and decoder stack self-attention and position-wise feed-forward layers. Positional encodings inject order, while masked decoder attention prevents access to future tokens.
experiments: Evaluates English-to-German and English-to-French translation on WMT 2014.
Compares translation quality, training cost, model variations, and constituency parsing transfer.
findings: The large model reports 28.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 English-to-German.
Attention-only computation substantially improves training parallelism.
limitations: Full self-attention has quadratic time and memory complexity in sequence length.
The original evaluation concentrates on translation and one parsing transfer task.
reproducibility: Requires WMT preprocessing, tokenizer/vocabulary settings, optimizer schedule, and model hyperparameters from the paper.
The paper specifies base and large configurations and training hardware/time.
open_questions: How can attention scale efficiently to much longer sequences?
Which positional representation generalizes best beyond training lengths?
evidence: Section 3 defines the architecture and attention mechanisms.
Table 2 reports translation quality and training cost.
Section 6 discusses model variations and parsing.
mermaid:
flowchart LR
  T[Tokens] --> E[Embeddings + Position]
  E --> M[Multi-Head Attention]
  M --> F[Feed Forward]
  F --> D[Decoder]
  D --> P[Output probabilities]
